text.title: logging-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for logging-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Log an error then immediately re-throw it (log-and-throw).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The same error is logged twice — once at the origin and once at the outer handler — creating duplicate entries that inflate error counts and confuse incident timelines.

failure-mode.description: Log passwords, tokens, authorization headers, or credit card numbers without masking.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Log aggregators and log shipping pipelines often have weaker access controls than primary storage. A single log export leaks all credentials.

failure-mode.description: Mix structured and unstructured log lines in the same service (some JSON, some plain text).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Log parsers must handle two formats. A single unstructured line breaks field-level alerting for the entire log stream.

failure-mode.description: Log entries with no request ID, user ID, or entity ID (no context).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Contextless logs cannot be correlated to a specific request or user. Debugging requires guessing from timestamps alone.

failure-mode.description: Build log message strings with f-strings or concatenation before passing to the logger.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The string is constructed even when the log level is filtered out. Pass structured key-value pairs — the logger skips serialization entirely for filtered levels.
